care_tips: Water when the top 2-3 inches of soil are dry. Wipe leaves with a damp cloth to remove dust and help photosynthesis.
Extremely drought-tolerant. Allow soil to dry out completely between waterings. Avoid overwatering to prevent root rot.
Requires consistent light. Rotate the plant 90 degrees every month. Water thoroughly when the top inch of soil feels dry.
Very hardy trailing plant. Water when leaves start to droop slightly. Trim vines regularly to encourage bushier growth.
Prefers well-draining soil. Susceptible to fluoride in tap water; use distilled or rainwater if leaf tips turn brown.
Succulent that needs sandy, well-draining soil. Water deeply but infrequently, allowing the soil to dry out completely.
Keep soil consistently moist but not soggy. Will tell you when it needs water by drooping its leaves. Mist regularly.
Thrives on neglect. Water only once a month or when soil is completely dry. Keep away from direct hot sunlight.
Thrives in high humidity. Keep soil damp at all times and mist daily or place on a pebble tray with water.
Classic succulent. Water only when the leaves feel slightly soft or puckered. Place in a south-facing window.
Prefers cooler temperatures. Water thoroughly and then let the top half of the soil dry out before watering again.
Requires high humidity and purified water. Keep soil evenly moist. Sensitive to drafts and dramatic temperature changes.
Water when the top 2 inches of soil are dry. Mist leaves in dry seasons. Avoid moving the plant to prevent leaf drop.
Needs excellent drainage. Water sparingly and avoid getting water on the spherical leaves to prevent rot.
Trailing or climbing vine. Provide a moss pole for support. Keep soil moist but not wet.
Extremely tough. Tolerates low light, poor soil, and irregular watering. Wipe leaves occasionally to keep clean.
Easy to grow. Prefers warm temperatures and moderate humidity. Allow soil to dry out slightly between waterings.
Requires ample sunlight to bloom. Keep soil moist during spring and summer, and let it dry out more in winter.
Water once a week by soaking the bark mix and draining completely. Use orchid-specific fertilizer monthly.
Keep soil moist but not soggy. Leaves fold up at night, mimicking hands in prayer. Prefers warm, humid rooms.
Prefers an environment with bright indirect light and moderate water. Provide well-drained potting mix and feed with a balanced fertilizer during the active growing season.
Prefers an environment with partial shade / bright indirect light and moderate water. Provide well-drained potting mix and feed with a balanced fertilizer during the active growing season.
Prefers an environment with full sun / direct light and moderate water. Provide well-drained potting mix and feed with a balanced fertilizer during the active growing season.
